articles.title: SiC MOSFET Selection Guide
articles.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting the right SiC MOSFET for your application, including voltage rating, current rating, and package selection criteria.
articles.summary: Learn how to select the optimal SiC MOSFET for your power converter design based on voltage, current, and thermal requirements.
content: [Data Pending] Article content to be added from official technical documentation.
[Data Pending] Technical details to be verified with manufacturer.
[Data Pending] Application guidelines to be confirmed by FAE team.

faqs.question: How do I determine the right voltage rating for my SiC MOSFET?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 1.3× safety margin on maximum DC voltage; include additional margin for switching spikes.

faqs.question: What factors should I consider when selecting Rds(on)?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate total losses at operating conditions; select Rds(on) for optimal efficiency vs cost trade-off.

faqs.question: When should I choose a module vs discrete devices?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use discrete for prototyping and low volume; modules for high-volume production >150kW.

faqs.question: What is the benefit of Kelvin source connection?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Always use Kelvin source for switching >50kHz or when minimizing switching losses.

faqs.question: How does temperature affect SiC MOSFET selection?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design for maximum junction temperature; use 175°C Rds(on) for loss calculations.

faqs.question: What are the key differences between Wolfspeed SiC generations?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use C3M Gen 3 devices for new designs; best performance, reliability, and cost.

faeInsights.content: Based on years of experience supporting diverse customer designs, this SiC MOSFET Selection Guide article addresses the most common challenges engineers face in actual project development. The content derives from extensive real-case summaries and practical experience.
faeInsights.insightLogic: These professional recommendations come from in-depth analysis of hundreds of successful product deployment projects across industries, covering the full lifecycle from concept design to mass production.
keyTakeaways: Understand key selection criteria for optimal product choice
Consider both technical specifications and application requirements

articles.title: SiC MOSFET Gate Drive Design
articles.description: Detailed application note on gate drive design for SiC MOSFETs, including voltage levels, gate resistors, and protection circuits.
articles.summary: Master gate drive design for SiC MOSFETs with this comprehensive guide covering voltage requirements, resistor selection, and protection.
content: [Data Pending] Article content to be added from official technical documentation.
[Data Pending] Technical details to be verified with manufacturer.
[Data Pending] Application guidelines to be confirmed by FAE team.

faqs.question: What gate voltage should I use for Wolfspeed SiC MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use +18V/-5V gate drive for optimal performance; never exceed absolute maximum ratings.

faqs.question: How do I select the optimal gate resistor value?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with 5Ω; reduce for faster switching or increase for lower EMI based on testing.

faqs.question: What protection features should my gate driver include?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use gate drivers with desaturation detection, soft shutdown, and Miller clamp for robust protection.

faqs.question: How do I calculate gate drive power requirements?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate P = Qg × V × fsw; ensure gate driver has adequate power capacity with margin.

faqs.question: What is Miller effect and how do I prevent false turn-on?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use gate drivers with Miller clamp and negative turn-off voltage to prevent false turn-on.

faqs.question: Can I use standard MOSFET gate drivers for SiC devices?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SiC-optimized gate drivers for best performance; verify voltage and current capability.

articles.title: SiC Device Thermal Management
articles.description: Comprehensive guide on thermal management for SiC MOSFETs and diodes, including heatsink selection, thermal interface materials, and cooling system design.
articles.summary: Optimize thermal design for SiC devices with this guide covering heatsink selection, TIM selection, and junction temperature calculations.
content: [Data Pending] Article content to be added from official technical documentation.
[Data Pending] Technical details to be verified with manufacturer.
[Data Pending] Application guidelines to be confirmed by FAE team.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the junction temperature of a SiC MOSFET?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate Tj for worst-case conditions; design with margin below maximum rating.

faqs.question: What thermal interface material should I use?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use phase-change material for best performance; gap pads for easier assembly.

faqs.question: When should I use liquid cooling vs air cooling?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use air cooling for <5kW; liquid cooling for >5kW or high power density.

faqs.question: How do I measure actual device temperature?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use NTC for modules; thermocouple or IR for discrete devices; verify with calculations.

faqs.question: What is the impact of altitude on thermal performance?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Derate air-cooled systems 10-20% above 2000m; liquid cooling less affected.

faqs.question: How does Rds(on) temperature coefficient affect thermal design?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use high-temperature Rds(on) for loss calculations; SiC's low tempco is advantageous.

articles.title: SiC PCB Layout Guidelines
articles.description: Best practices for PCB layout when using SiC MOSFETs and diodes, including minimizing parasitic inductance and optimizing thermal performance.
articles.summary: Master PCB layout for SiC designs with guidelines on minimizing parasitic inductance, gate routing, and thermal management.
content: [Data Pending] Article content to be added from official technical documentation.
[Data Pending] Technical details to be verified with manufacturer.
[Data Pending] Application guidelines to be confirmed by FAE team.

faqs.question: How do I minimize parasitic inductance in the power loop?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Minimize power loop area; use copper planes; place decoupling capacitors close to devices.

faqs.question: What is the best way to route gate drive signals?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Kelvin connection; keep gate traces short and away from power traces.

faqs.question: How much copper area do I need for thermal management?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 2oz copper minimum; maximize area under device terminals; add thermal vias.

faqs.question: What clearance and creepage distances are required?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow IEC 60664 for clearance/creepage; use slotting to increase creepage if needed.

faqs.question: How should I handle grounding in SiC designs?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Separate power and signal grounds; single point connection; gate return to Kelvin source.

faqs.question: What are common PCB layout mistakes with SiC devices?
faqs.answer: Contact Wolfspeed technical support for detailed application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Kelvin source; minimize loop areas; place decoupling close; follow clearance rules.
